- Bxf7+ Rxf7 (rook f8 takes f7): then Rxf7? White rook d7 takes f7. Hmm.
- Bxf7+ Kxf7: king takes bishop. Then Qf3+...?

After Bxf7+, black has options:
1. Rxf7 (rook from f8): then Rxf7 (Rd7xf7) ... and black? Actually after Bxf7+ Rxf7, white can play Rxf7 Kxf7 and then? Hmm.

Wait, the move Bxf7+ gives check (bishop on f7 attacks g8 king? f7 to g8 is diagonal, yes). So black must respond to check.

Gained f7 pawn (1), f8 rook (5). Net +3. But black to move after Rxf7 (it's black's turn? let's recount).

But the position might have more.

What about Rxf7 directly? Rd7xf7. Rxf7 — does it give check? Rook on f7 attacks g7 bishop, f8 rook, and along rank. Not check to Kg8. Hmm. But after Rxf7, black? Bxf7? Bg7 doesn't attack f7. After Bxf7+ Kxf7 (king takes bishop), is f7 protected? White Qf3 still protects f7? Wait sequence: 25.Rxf7 (rook d7 takes f7 pawn) Rxf7 (rook f8 takes white rook f7) Bxf7+ (bishop c4 takes rook f7, check). Now black king g8 in check from Bf7 (f7-g8 diagonal). Kxf7? Is f7 protected by white? Qf3 attacks f7 (f-file clear: f3-f4-f5-f6-f7, yes). So Kxf7 illegal. Other? Re8 can block? Check from bishop f7 to g8 — adjacent diagonal, cannot block. Must move king or capture. Kxf7 illegal (Qf3). King moves: Kh8? g8 to h8 — is h8 attacked? Bf7 attacks h7? f7-g8 (king), h? Bishop f7 attacks g8, h? f7 diagonal: g8 (king), h? beyond. e8 (rook), d? Let's see bishop on f7: diagonals: g8 (king), e8 (black rook e8), g6 (black pawn), e6, d5, c4 (empty now), b3 (white pawn), and h? f7-h? f7 to h9 off. Other diagonal: f7-e6-d5-c4-b3(white pawn)-a2(white pawn); f7-g6 (black pawn) stops; f7-e8 (black rook) stops. So bishop f7 gives check along f7-g8. King moves: Kh8 — attacked by?
